mission.title: flagger: Support for istio's warmupDurationSecs
mission.description: Support for istio's warmupDurationSecs. Requested by 15+ users.

steps.title: Check current flagger deployment
steps.description:
Verify your flagger version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n flagger -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flagger
helm list -n flagger 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working flagger installation.

steps.title: Review flagger configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant flagger configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n flagger -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flagger
kubectl get configmap -n flagger -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=flagger
```
## Describe the feature

Istio [added](https://github.com/istio/api/pull/2153) in 2022 the ability to "warm up" traffic slowly with a new load balancer property called `warmupDurationSecs`

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support for istio's warmupDurationSecs
steps.description:
If I'm doing it wrong, please point it out to me.
```yaml
...
    trafficPolicy:
      loadBalancer:
        simple: LEAST_REQUEST
        warmupDurationSecs: 30s
...
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n flagger -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flagger
kubectl get events -n flagger --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Support for istio's warmupDurationSecs" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: See the linked issue and PR for the community-verified solution.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with flagger installed or the issue environment reproducible.
